name: Starsky Robotics
tagline: Autonomous trucking startup that combined highway self-driving with remote teleoperation
description: Founded in 2016 by Stefan Seltz-Axmacher and Kartik Tiwari, Starsky Robotics raised $21.5M to build autonomous trucks that used remote human teleoperation for complex maneuvers. The company completed the first fully unmanned truck run on a public highway in 2019, but shut down in early 2020 after its $20M Series B fell through amid declining investor enthusiasm for autonomous vehicle technology and a trucking industry recession.
story: Starsky Robotics had a thesis that was equal parts pragmatic and audacious: autonomous trucks didn't need to handle every scenario if a remote human operator could take over for the hard parts. Founded in 2016 by Stefan Seltz-Axmacher and Kartik Tiwari, the company raised $21.5 million and built a hybrid system combining highway self-driving with teleoperation for exits, loading docks, and tricky maneuvers. In June 2019, Starsky pulled off a genuine milestone — the first fully unmanned truck run on a public highway, with no safety driver in the cab. But milestones don't pay bills. A trucking industry recession crushed freight rates, and investor appetite for autonomous vehicles was cooling after years of overblown timelines from the sector's biggest players. When Starsky's $20 million Series B fell through in early 2020, the 30-person team had no runway left. Seltz-Axmacher published a remarkably candid postmortem, arguing that the entire autonomous vehicle industry had a 'supervised learning problem' — the technology was perpetually almost-good-enough, and investors had grown tired of waiting.

sources.title: The End of Starsky Robotics — Founder postmortem by Stefan Seltz-Axmacher
Self-Driving Truck Startup Starsky Robotics Shuts Down After Series B Falls Through
Starsky Robotics ends remote truck experiment, shuts down operations
